_meta.description: All review module questions with structured response types for UI modal design

Single-select.description: Radio button list, one selection only

_meta.moduleStructure: 23 category modules × 100 questions each (10 sections × 10 questions) + Main Module (100Q: 34 Demographics + 33 DNW + 33 Must Haves) + General Questions (50Q) + Trade-off Questions (50Q) = 2,600 total questions

modules.structure: 3 sections — Demographics (34Q), Do Not Wants (33Q), Must Haves (33Q)

questions.question: What is your nationality or primary citizenship?
Do you hold dual or multiple citizenships? If so, which countries?
What is your current country of residence?
What is your age range?
What is your current relationship status? (Select one: single, in a relationship, married, domestic partnership, divorced, widowed)
Will your partner/spouse relocate with you?
Does your partner/spouse need work authorization in the new location?
Do you have children?
What are your children's age ranges? (Select all that apply: infant 0-2, toddler 3-5, elementary 6-10, middle school 11-13, high school 14-17, adult 18+)
Will all children relocate with you?
Do any of your children have special educational or medical needs?
What is your highest level of education completed? (Select one: high school, trade/vocational, associate's, bachelor's, master's, doctorate, professional degree)
Are you involved in a skilled trade? If so, what specific trade certification do you hold?
Are you currently or formerly in the military?
Do you need access to military or veteran services in your new location?
What is your current employment status? (Select one: employed full-time, employed part-time, self-employed, business owner, retired, unemployed, student)
Are you seeking employment in your new location, or will you work remotely/be self-employed? (Select one: seeking local employment, remote work, self-employed, business owner, retired, undecided)
What is your primary industry or professional field?
What is your preferred work arrangement? (Select one: fully remote, hybrid, in-office, freelance, retired)
What is your annual household income (USD equivalent)?
What percentage of your current income could you maintain if you relocated internationally?
What is your current housing situation? (Select one: own, rent, live with family, other)
What is your current monthly housing cost (USD equivalent)?
How many bedrooms do you need in your new home?
What type of area do you currently live in? (Select one: urban core, suburban, small town, rural)
What is the approximate population of your current city or town?
Do you have any chronic health conditions or special medical requirements?

What specific medical conditions require ongoing treatment or specialist access? (Select all that apply: cardiovascular, respiratory, diabetes, autoimmune, neurological, orthopedic, mental health, cancer/oncology, none)
What is your primary mode of daily transportation today? (Select one: personal car, public transit, bicycle, walking, ride-hailing, motorcycle, combination)
Do you have pets that would relocate with you?
What type and how many pets do you have? (Select all that apply: dog(s), cat(s), bird(s), reptile(s), fish, small mammals, exotic pets, none)
Are any of your pets a breed that faces import restrictions in certain countries?
How many languages do you speak fluently, and which ones?
What is your relocation timeline? (Select one: immediate, 6 months, 1-2 years, 2+ years, exploring/no timeline)
How much do you want to avoid extreme heat (regularly over 35C / 95F)?
How much do you want to avoid extreme cold (regularly below -10C / 14F)?
How much do you want to avoid high humidity and tropical climate conditions?
How much do you want to avoid significant natural disaster risks (earthquakes, hurricanes, floods, wildfires)?
How much do you want to avoid severe air pollution (regularly unhealthy air quality)?
How much do you want to avoid severe water quality or water shortage issues?
How much do you want to avoid language barriers you cannot overcome?
How much do you want to avoid living under restrictive social or religious laws?
How much do you want to avoid a lack of diversity or a monocultural environment?
How much do you want to avoid hostile attitudes toward foreigners or immigrants?
How much do you want to avoid gender-based or LGBTQ+ discrimination?
How much do you want to avoid religious intolerance or persecution?
How much do you want to avoid a poor or inaccessible healthcare system?
How much do you want to avoid unreliable infrastructure (power outages, poor roads, inconsistent utilities)?
How much do you want to avoid poor internet and telecommunications reliability?
How much do you want to avoid high taxes without adequate public services in return?
How much do you want to avoid a high cost of living that is not matched by quality of life?
How much do you want to avoid an unstable economy and volatile currency?
How much do you want to avoid living under an authoritarian or non-democratic government?
How much do you want to avoid systemic corruption and an unreliable legal system?
How much do you want to avoid political instability or civil unrest?
How much do you want to avoid high crime rates and personal safety concerns?
How much do you want to avoid a lack of personal safety for women and minorities?
How much do you want to avoid complex visa or residency complications?
How much do you want to avoid a car-dependent lifestyle with no viable alternatives?
How much do you want to avoid poor public transportation options?
How much do you want to avoid a lack of career or professional opportunities?
How much do you want to avoid lacking access to specific foods, products, or dietary needs?
How much do you want to avoid isolation from family and close friends?
How much do you want to avoid limited education options for your children?
How much do you want to avoid limited access to international airports and travel connectivity?
How much do you want to avoid excessive noise pollution or severe overcrowding?
How much do you want to avoid a lack of outdoor recreation, parks, and green spaces?
How important is it that English (or your primary language) is widely spoken?
How important is a welcoming attitude toward newcomers and foreigners?
How important is having job opportunities available in your professional field?
How important is the ability to legally work in your new location?
How important is a reasonable and affordable cost of living?
How important is affordable housing availability (rent or purchase)?
How important is a stable economy and reliable currency?
How important is strong legal protection for foreigners and property rights?
How important is access to quality healthcare and medical facilities?
How important is a low crime rate and strong personal safety?
How important is political stability and democratic governance?
How important is a clean environment (air, water, streets)?
How important is good air quality for daily life and outdoor activities?
How important is reliable high-speed internet and modern telecommunications?
How important is reliable utilities infrastructure (electricity, water, gas)?
How important is good public transportation and transit options?
How important are walkable neighborhoods where daily needs are nearby?
How important is proximity to an international airport with good connections?
How important is access to outdoor recreation, nature, and green spaces?
How important is a vibrant food scene with diverse dining options?
How important is access to cultural activities (museums, theater, arts)?
How important is access to fitness facilities and sports infrastructure?
How important is nightlife and entertainment options?
How important is a good education system (for you or your children)?
How important is access to professional networking and career development?
How important is a family-friendly environment with child-oriented resources?
How important is an expat or international community in your new location?
How important is access to your religious or spiritual community?
How important is a pet-friendly environment (parks, vets, pet-friendly housing)?
How important is a climate and weather pattern that suits your lifestyle preferences?
How important is the overall happiness and life satisfaction of residents in your prospective city?
How important is it that the community actively embraces diversity and inclusion?
How important is proximity to family, friends, or an existing support network?
Who is the primary decision-maker for this relocation? (Select one: myself alone, myself and partner equally, primarily my partner, family consensus, other)
How aligned is your partner/household on relocation priorities?
What regions, countries, or cities are you most drawn to — and which have you already visited or lived in?
Do you have family obligations that affect where you can relocate?
What type of family obligations do you have, and how often must you travel for them?
How do you approach risk in life decisions? (Select one: very cautious, somewhat cautious, balanced, somewhat adventurous, very adventurous)
How do you handle uncertainty and change? (Select one: need stability, prefer predictability, adaptable, thrive on change, seek disruption)

What is the PRIMARY reason driving your relocation? (Select one: career opportunity, lifestyle change, retirement, cost savings, family reunification, escape/fresh start, adventure, relationship, health/climate, safety)
What is your overall risk tolerance for major life changes?
What is your openness to lifestyle experimentation?
What pace of life do you prefer? (Select one: very slow/relaxed, slow/easygoing, moderate, fast-paced, intense/high-energy)
What is your preferred social style? (Select one: mostly solitary, small intimate circle, moderate social life, very social/outgoing, extroverted/always connecting)
What is your international living experience? (Select one: never lived abroad, short stays only <6 months, lived abroad 1-3 years, lived abroad 3+ years, serial expat/multiple countries)
Which cities or countries have you visited or lived in that you LOVED, and which did you hate — and specifically WHY?
If you could split your monthly budget any way you wanted, what percentage would go to housing, food, entertainment, travel, savings, and healthcare?

What are your biggest relocation fears? (Select all that apply: loneliness/isolation, language barriers, career setback, financial loss, culture shock, safety concerns, healthcare access, missing family, bureaucracy, failure/regret)
Do you prefer the familiar or the adventurous?
What is your tolerance for cultural differences?
What is your comfort level with language barriers?
Do you prefer expat communities or full local integration?
What is your expected timeline for feeling socially integrated? (Select one: 1-3 months, 3-6 months, 6-12 months, 1-2 years, 2+ years)
How interested are you in cultural bridge-building and exchange?
How willing are you to adapt to different social norms?
How would you describe your ideal social environment? (Select one: quiet/private, small close-knit community, active neighborhood, bustling urban life, cosmopolitan hub)
How comfortable are you attending large social events versus intimate gatherings?
What specific communities, hobbies, or subcultures do you NEED access to in order to feel fulfilled?
How important is it that your neighborhood has people in a similar life stage to yours (young professionals, families, retirees)?
How private or open do you prefer to be within your community?

What social support systems do you need to thrive? (Select all that apply: religious community, professional network, parent groups, hobby/interest clubs, expat community, mental health support, volunteer organizations, none specific)
How important is building local community roots versus maintaining a transient/flexible lifestyle?
What is your stance on work-life balance?
How do you prefer to spend your weekends? (Select one: relaxing at home, outdoor activities, social events, cultural exploration, travel/day trips, mix of everything)
What is your ideal noise level environment?
What is your ideal community size? (Select one: village <5,000, small town 5K-50K, mid-size city 50K-500K, large city 500K-2M, mega city 2M+)
How do seasonal changes affect your mood and activity level?
How important is environmental sustainability to your lifestyle?
What are your long-term settlement plans? (Select one: permanent relocation, 5-10 years, 2-5 years, 1-2 years, exploring/undecided)
Are your intentions temporary or permanent? (Select one: definitely permanent, leaning permanent, truly undecided, leaning temporary, definitely temporary)
How important is having an exit strategy or reversible plan?
What is your retirement timeline consideration? (Select one: already retired, retiring within 5 years, 5-15 years, 15+ years, not a factor)
How would you define success after 2 years in your new location?
What is your settling period tolerance — how long before you expect to feel at home? (Select one: 1-3 months, 3-6 months, 6-12 months, 1-2 years, 2+ years)
What is your backup plan approach if things don't work out? (Select one: return home, try another country, move to a different city, adapt and stay, no backup plan)
What is your biggest hope for your new life?
Tell us your relocation story: What's driving your desire to move, and what would "success" look like for you?
Describe your perfect ordinary Tuesday in your ideal new location — morning to evening, where do you live, work, eat, and spend your time?
What specific communities, interests, or activities are absolutely central to your identity and happiness that we haven't asked about?
What are your biggest fears or concerns about relocating, and what past experiences (positive or negative) with places you've lived should we know about?
What would make you feel "at home" in your new location?
If you had to explain to a close friend why certain places would be absolutely wrong for you, and others would be perfect, what would you tell them that our questionnaire might have missed?
Would you accept a higher cost of living for significantly better personal safety and lower crime?
Would you live in a less exciting city (fewer restaurants, nightlife, culture) if it meant your family felt completely safe?
Would you accept stricter laws and less personal freedom in exchange for a very low crime rate and political stability?
Would you move to a city with moderate safety concerns if it had world-class healthcare within walking distance?
Would you choose a safe but isolated small town over a vibrant but riskier large city?
Would you live in a country with mild political instability if it offered significantly better weather and cost of living?
Would you pay 30-50% more in monthly expenses to live in a city with excellent infrastructure, healthcare, and services?
Would you accept a smaller home or apartment to live in a walkable neighborhood with everything nearby?
Would you choose a cheaper city with average food options over an expensive city with an incredible food scene?
Would you sacrifice access to international-standard shopping and services for a significantly lower cost of living?
Would you accept higher taxes in exchange for excellent public services (healthcare, transit, education, safety)?
Would you live further from the city center to afford a larger home, even if it meant a longer commute?
Would you tolerate a climate you dislike (too hot, too cold, too humid) for significantly better career opportunities?
Would you accept fewer sunshine days for a city with a stronger professional network in your field?
Would you choose perfect weather in a rural area over imperfect weather in a thriving metropolis?
Would you live in a city with harsh winters if it had the best education options for your children?
Would you sacrifice beach access for mountain access (or vice versa) if one option had better overall infrastructure?
Would you accept a climate with natural disaster risk (earthquakes, hurricanes) for a city that excels in everything else?
Would you take a significant pay cut to live in a city with a dramatically better quality of life?
Would you give up career advancement opportunities to live in a place that perfectly matches your lifestyle values?
Would you accept a less favorable tax situation for a city that has everything else you want?
Would you work in a different time zone (inconvenient hours) to live in your dream location?
Would you choose a city with fewer professional networking opportunities if it had a stronger sense of community?
Would you sacrifice financial growth potential for immediate quality of life improvement?
Would you live in a place where you don't speak the language if everything else was perfect?
Would you choose a culturally unfamiliar environment over a comfortable expat bubble if it meant deeper integration?
Would you sacrifice proximity to family and friends for a location that matches all other priorities?
Would you accept a less diverse community for a city that scores perfectly on safety, cost, and climate?
Would you live somewhere with limited nightlife and entertainment if the daytime lifestyle was exceptional?
Would you choose a city where you'd be a visible minority if it was the best match on every other metric?
Would you accept a longer commute to hospitals and specialists for a location with better overall daily life quality?
Would you live in a country with excellent public healthcare but less personal freedom?
Would you sacrifice access to cutting-edge medical technology for a city with better air quality, less stress, and a healthier lifestyle?
Would you accept higher out-of-pocket healthcare costs for a location with better weather and outdoor activity options?
Would you choose a location with fewer wellness amenities (gyms, spas, yoga) if it had stronger community health outcomes?
Would you live further from nature and outdoor recreation to be closer to top-tier medical facilities?
Would you accept a less desirable home (older, smaller, fewer amenities) to live in the perfect neighborhood?
Would you rent instead of own to live in a city you love, even if you could afford to buy in a city you only like?
Would you sacrifice modern urban design and architecture for a historic, charming neighborhood with older infrastructure?
Would you accept a noisier, busier neighborhood for walkability and proximity to everything?
Would you choose a city with less green space but better public transit over one with beautiful parks but car-dependent living?
Would you accept a less aesthetically pleasing environment (concrete, industrial) for a city with exceptional community and culture?
Would you accept more bureaucracy and slower government services for a country with stronger civil liberties?
Would you live in a more religiously conservative area if it had the best family environment and lowest cost?
Would you sacrifice access to certain substances (alcohol, cannabis) for a safer, more family-friendly environment?
Would you accept internet censorship or limited media access for a location that excels in other areas?
Would you choose a location with weaker environmental regulations but stronger economic opportunities?
Would you accept limited pet-friendliness (fewer parks, stricter rules) for a city that otherwise perfectly matches your needs?
Would you live in a location with limited cultural heritage and traditions if it was modern, efficient, and comfortable?
Looking at everything you've told us — if you could ONLY optimize for THREE things in your new home and had to accept "good enough" on everything else, what would your three be?

modules.structure: 10 sections × 10 questions, each ending with a ranking question + 1 final master ranking (Q100)

questions.question: How important is access to world-class art museums in your ideal location?

What types of museums are most important to you? (Select all that apply: fine art, contemporary art, natural history, science/technology, history/cultural, photography, design/architecture, children's museums, military/war, ethnographic)
How frequently do you visit museums or galleries?
How important are special exhibitions and rotating displays versus permanent collections?
How willing are you to pay premium prices for exceptional museum access (annual memberships, special events)?
How important is English-language accessibility in museums and galleries?
What type of gallery scene appeals to you most? (Select one: blue-chip/established, emerging/experimental, community, street art/urban, commercial, mixed)
How important is it that museums reflect diverse global cultures, not just Western art?
How important is the architectural significance of museum buildings themselves?
Rank these museum factors from most to least important: Museum world-class quality, Exhibition variety, Gallery scene, Cultural diversity, English accessibility
How important is access to live theater in your ideal location?

What types of theatrical performances interest you? (Select all that apply: Broadway/West End-style musicals, dramatic plays, opera, ballet, modern dance, experimental theater, comedy/improv, cabaret, puppetry, circus arts)
How important is access to live music venues of various sizes?

What types of live music venues appeal to you? (Select all that apply: concert halls, intimate jazz clubs, rock/indie venues, outdoor amphitheaters, stadium concerts, DJ/electronic clubs, busking/street music, opera houses)
How frequently do you attend live performances (theater, music, dance)?
How important are internationally touring productions and world-famous artists visiting your city?
What is your typical monthly entertainment budget for performing arts?
How important is English-language accessibility for performances?
How willing are you to attend performances in the local language as part of cultural immersion? CULTURAL FLEX
Rank these performing arts factors from most to least important: Theater access, Live music venues, International touring acts, Performance variety, Language accessibility
How important is being part of an active creative community?

What types of creative communities interest you? (Select all that apply: visual arts, music/bands, writing/literary, film/video, photography, theater/acting, dance, crafts/maker spaces, digital arts, culinary arts)
What level of creative community engagement do you prefer? (Select one: observer/audience, casual participant, active member, professional practitioner, community leader)
How important are artist studios and creative coworking spaces?

What type of arts education and workshops interest you? (Select all that apply: painting/drawing, pottery/sculpture, music lessons, dance classes, writing workshops, photography, digital media, film production, acting, crafts)
How important are cultural mentorship and apprenticeship opportunities?
How important is access to public funding, grants, or residency programs that support local artists?
How important is the presence of independent bookstores, art-house cinemas, and alternative cultural spaces?
How important is a thriving street art, public art installation, and open-air cultural scene?
Rank these creative community factors from most to least important: Creative community access, Studios/workspaces, Arts education, Public art scene, Arts funding/grants
How important are annual cultural festivals in your ideal location?

What types of cultural festivals interest you? (Select all that apply: music festivals, film festivals, literary festivals, food/wine festivals, art fairs, theater festivals, comedy festivals, heritage festivals, design weeks, fashion weeks)
What scale of cultural events appeals to you most? (Select one: intimate/local, neighborhood/community, city-wide, national/international, mega-events)
How frequently do you want major cultural events in your area?
How important is international cultural programming (foreign film series, world music, international art exhibitions)?
How important are free and accessible cultural events for the public?
How important are outdoor cultural events and venues (open-air cinema, park concerts, sculpture gardens)?
How important are cultural events that celebrate local and regional history and traditions?
How important are cultural events that address contemporary social issues (social justice art, political theater, documentary screenings)?
Rank these cultural events factors from most to least important: Festival variety, Event frequency, International programming, Free/accessible events, Local tradition celebrations
How important is access to art-house and independent cinemas?
How important is a strong local film and documentary scene?
How important is access to a vibrant literary culture (bookstores, author events, book clubs, literary readings)?
How important is access to public libraries with diverse collections and programming?
How important is local media and journalism quality (newspapers, magazines, podcasts, blogs)?
How important is access to international media and publications in English or your language?
How important is a strong podcast and digital media creator community?
How important is access to foreign language films with subtitles?
How willing are you to engage with local-language media as part of cultural integration? CULTURAL FLEX
Rank these media/literary factors from most to least important: Independent cinema, Literary culture, Public libraries, Local media quality, International media access
How important is living in a city with significant architectural heritage?
How important is contemporary architecture and innovative urban design in your destination?
How important is access to design museums, architecture tours, and built-environment exhibitions?
How important is a strong interior design and home décor retail scene?
How important is living in a city known for fashion and style?
How important is access to design festivals, architecture biennales, or design weeks?
How important is the quality of public space design (plazas, parks, waterfronts, pedestrian areas)?
How important is the preservation of historic buildings and neighborhoods?
How willing are you to appreciate and adapt to a very different architectural and design aesthetic from your home country? CULTURAL FLEX
Rank these architecture/design factors from most to least important: Architectural heritage, Contemporary design, Public space quality, Historic preservation, Fashion/design scene
How important is a vibrant and diverse music scene in your destination?

Which music genres are most important to have access to? (Select all that apply: classical, jazz, rock/indie, electronic/DJ, hip-hop, world music, folk/traditional, Latin, R&B/soul, metal, pop, country)
How important is access to record stores, music equipment shops, and vinyl culture?
How important is a strong DJ and electronic music scene (clubs, raves, festivals)?
How important are music education opportunities (lessons, conservatories, jam sessions)?
How important is a city known for producing or nurturing musical talent?
How important are pre- and post-performance dining and social options near cultural venues?
How important is children's and family-friendly arts and entertainment programming?
How willing are you to explore and appreciate the local music traditions of your destination? CULTURAL FLEX
Rank these music/nightlife factors from most to least important: Music scene diversity, Live venue quality, Genre variety, Music education, Local music traditions
How important is cultural integration for your overall relocation success?

What types of cultural learning opportunities interest you? (Select all that apply: language classes, cooking classes, history courses, art workshops, local customs orientation, cultural immersion programs, volunteer programs, mentorship)
How important is maintaining your own cultural and artistic traditions after relocating?
How important are cultural institutions that serve expatriate communities (international clubs, expat arts groups)?
How important are multilingual cultural resources and programming?
How important is a city that values intellectual discourse, debate, and academic culture?
How important is cultural education for personal growth and lifelong learning?
How important are cultural approaches to work-life balance (long lunches, afternoon siestas, early evenings)?
How willing are you to fully immerse yourself in the local cultural scene rather than staying in expat bubbles? CULTURAL FLEX
Rank these integration factors from most to least important: Cultural learning, Maintaining own traditions, Expat community, Intellectual culture, Local immersion
How willing are you to accept a smaller arts scene in exchange for lower cost of living?
How willing are you to accept limited English-language cultural programming?
How willing are you to travel (1-2 hours) to access major cultural institutions?
How willing are you to accept a more traditional cultural scene versus cutting-edge contemporary?
How willing are you to pay higher cost of living for proximity to world-class cultural institutions?
How willing are you to accept a strong cultural scene that is primarily in a language you don't speak?
How important is it to live in a city on the "cultural world map" (known internationally for arts)?
What is your approximate annual budget for arts and cultural activities (memberships, tickets, classes)?
How willing are you to build cultural community from scratch in a less-established arts scene?
Rank these trade-off factors from most to least important: Arts scene size vs. cost, Language accessibility, Travel distance, Traditional vs. contemporary, Cultural world reputation

Which arts and culture issues are absolute dealbreakers that would eliminate a destination? (Select all that apply: no museums, no live music, censored arts, no theaters, no cultural festivals, no English programming, no bookstores/libraries, no creative community)

Which arts features are non-negotiable must-haves? (Select all that apply: world-class museums, live music venues, theater scene, cultural festivals, independent cinema, bookstores/libraries, creative community, public art, arts education)
On a scale of 0-100, how would you rank arts and culture as a priority compared to all other relocation factors?
How important is it that your destination's arts and cultural scene is growing and improving?
How important is government support and investment in arts and culture?
How important is the social aspect of cultural activities (meeting people, building friendships through arts)?
What type of cultural legacy or contribution would you like to make in your new community? (Select one: audience member, participant, organizer, patron/donor, creator, educator, no preference)
How willing are you to accept a culturally different arts scene than what you're used to? CULTURAL FLEX
How important are cultural approaches to aging, life transitions, and community belonging?

Rank these overall arts & culture categories from most to least important for your relocation: Museums & galleries, Theater & performing arts, Creative community, Cultural events & festivals, Film & literary culture, Architecture & design, Music & nightlife, Cultural integration, Arts trade-offs, Overall cultural investment

modules.structure: 10 sections x 10 questions, each ending with a ranking question + 1 final master ranking (Q100)

questions.question: What is your ideal daytime temperature range for most of the year?
What is the maximum summer temperature you can comfortably tolerate on a regular basis?
What is the minimum winter temperature you can comfortably tolerate?
How important is it that temperatures stay consistent year-round (tropical stability) versus having distinct seasonal shifts?
Do you have medical conditions (arthritis, Raynaud's, heat sensitivity, MS) affected by temperature extremes?
How important is reliable home heating and air conditioning infrastructure in your destination?
How does extreme heat (above 38C/100F) affect your ability to work, exercise, and go about daily life?
How does extreme cold (below 0C/32F) affect your ability to work, exercise, and go about daily life?
Would you accept higher energy costs for a climate that requires year-round heating or cooling?
Rank these temperature factors from most to least important: Average daily comfort range, Summer heat tolerance, Winter cold tolerance, Temperature consistency, Climate-related health needs
What is your ideal relative humidity range?
How does high humidity (above 70%) affect your daily comfort and energy levels?
How does very low humidity (below 20%) affect your skin, sinuses, and respiratory health?
Do you have allergies or asthma that are worsened by specific humidity levels?
How concerned are you about mold, mildew, and moisture damage to your home in humid climates?
Would you use a dehumidifier or humidifier daily to manage indoor air?
How does humidity affect your outdoor exercise and activity levels?
How important is a dry climate for preserving personal belongings (electronics, instruments, art, clothing)?
How do you feel about the "feels like" temperature when humidity makes it significantly hotter than actual temperature?
Rank these humidity factors from most to least important: Daily comfort level, Health and respiratory impact, Home and property protection, Outdoor activity impact, "Feels like" heat index
How many rainy days per month are you comfortable with?
Do you prefer brief intense rain showers (tropical style) or long drizzly periods (maritime style)?
How important is having a predictable dry season for planning outdoor activities and travel?
Could you live in a city with a monsoon season (months of heavy daily rain)?
How do you feel about gray, overcast skies for extended periods (weeks without sunshine)?
Do you enjoy snow? How many months of snowfall would you accept?
How important is it that roads and infrastructure handle rain and snow effectively (drainage, plowing, de-icing)?
Would persistent rain or drizzle negatively affect your mental health and mood?
How important is annual sunshine hours in your destination (e.g., 300+ sunny days vs. 150)?
Rank these precipitation factors from most to least important: Total rainy days per year, Sunshine hours, Snow tolerance, Predictable dry season, Infrastructure quality for weather
Do you prefer four distinct seasons, two seasons (wet/dry), or year-round consistency?
How important is experiencing a traditional autumn with changing leaves and cooler temperatures?
How important is a warm, long summer for outdoor activities and social life?
How do you handle very short winter daylight hours (sunset at 4pm or earlier)?
How do you handle very long summer daylight hours (sunset at 10pm or later)?
Have you experienced or are you concerned about Seasonal Affective Disorder (SAD)?
How important is consistent daylight hours year-round (tropical locations near the equator)?
Do you need specific seasonal conditions for hobbies (skiing, surfing, gardening, cycling)?
How do seasonal transitions affect your wardrobe, energy costs, and daily routine?
Rank these seasonal factors from most to least important: Number of distinct seasons, Daylight hours consistency, Autumn/spring experience, Summer length and warmth, Seasonal hobby support
Which natural disasters are you most concerned about? (Select all that apply)
Would you live in a hurricane/typhoon/cyclone zone if housing was built to withstand them?
Would you live in an earthquake-prone area if building codes were strong?
How concerned are you about wildfire risk in your destination?
How concerned are you about flooding risk (coastal, river, flash flooding)?
Would you live in a tornado-prone region (e.g., U.S. Tornado Alley)?
How important is a robust early warning system for severe weather events?
How concerned are you about volcanic activity in your destination region?
Would natural disaster insurance costs (flood, earthquake, hurricane) affect your destination choice?
Rank these extreme weather factors from most to least important: Hurricane/typhoon risk, Earthquake risk, Wildfire risk, Flooding risk, Tornado risk
How important is clean air quality (low AQI) in your daily life?
What is the maximum Air Quality Index (AQI) you would tolerate on a regular basis?
Do you or family members have respiratory conditions (asthma, COPD) requiring clean air?
How concerned are you about seasonal smog, haze, or pollution events?
How important is low pollen count and allergen levels in your destination?
Would you avoid cities near heavy industrial areas or traffic pollution corridors?
How important is access to clean, drinkable tap water?
How concerned are you about sand storms or dust storms in arid climates?
Would you pay a premium to live in an area with certified clean air and low pollution?
Rank these air quality factors from most to least important: Average AQI level, Respiratory health impact, Pollen and allergens, Industrial pollution proximity, Clean water access
How many months per year do you need comfortable outdoor weather for walking, dining, and socializing?
Do you need year-round conditions for outdoor sports (running, cycling, swimming, tennis)?
How important is beach-friendly weather for a significant portion of the year?
Do you garden or grow food? How important is a long growing season?
How important is weather that supports an outdoor cafe and restaurant culture?

questions.question: How would you describe your religious or spiritual orientation? (Select one: Christianity, Islam, Judaism, Hinduism, Buddhism, Sikhism, Bahá'í, Jainism, Shinto, Taoism, Indigenous/traditional, spiritual but not religious, agnostic, atheist, secular humanist, other)

How often do you engage in personal prayer, meditation, or spiritual practice?
How frequently do you attend formal religious services or spiritual gatherings?
Do you consider yourself a practicing member of your faith or spiritual path?
How important is it that your beliefs are understood and respected by others in your community?
How do your religious or spiritual beliefs influence your moral and ethical decisions?
How comfortable are you openly discussing your beliefs with people of different faiths?
How do you view people of different religious or non-religious beliefs? (Select one: fully accepting, respectfully curious, tolerant but private, cautiously engaged, prefer like-minded community)
Rank these belief identity factors from most to least important: Centrality of faith, Practice frequency, Community respect, Interfaith comfort, Ethical influence
How important is having access to formal worship facilities (church, mosque, temple, synagogue, etc.) in your destination?

What type of worship facility do you need access to? (Select all that apply: church, mosque, synagogue, Hindu temple, Buddhist temple, Sikh gurdwara, meditation center, interfaith center, home-based worship, online/virtual, none needed)
How far are you willing to travel for regular worship services?
What size religious community do you prefer? (Select one: small intimate <50, medium 50-200, large 200-1000, mega 1000+, solitary practice, no preference)
How important is access to religious scholars, clergy, or spiritual advisors in your destination?
How important is having multilingual religious services available in your heritage language?
How important is the availability of religious education programs (Sunday school, Quran classes, Torah study, etc.)?
How important is the social fellowship aspect of your religious community?
How important are specific architectural and aesthetic features in your place of worship?
Rank these worship access factors from most to least important: Facility availability, Community size, Clergy access, Multilingual services, Religious education
How important is constitutional or legal protection of religious freedom in your destination?

Which specific legal protections are most important for your religious practice? (Select all that apply: freedom to worship, freedom to wear religious attire, freedom to proselytize, protection from discrimination, religious holiday accommodation, conscientious objection rights, dietary accommodation in institutions, religious marriage recognition)
How concerned are you about potential religious discrimination or persecution in your destination?
How important is it that you can freely express your religious identity in public (clothing, symbols, practices)?
How important is it that your children can freely practice your faith in school settings?
What level of religious influence in local government are you comfortable with?
How concerned are you about blasphemy laws, apostasy laws, or restrictions on religious expression?
How important is it that local businesses accommodate your religious needs (halal/kosher food, prayer times, Sabbath hours)?
How flexible are you about adapting your religious practices to local laws and customs while maintaining core beliefs? CULTURAL FLEX
Rank these religious freedom factors from most to least important: Legal protection, Anti-discrimination laws, Public expression freedom, School accommodation, Business accommodation
How important is living in a community with high religious tolerance and acceptance?
How comfortable are you living in a community where the dominant religion is different from yours?

How do you prefer to handle religious differences with neighbors and community members? (Select one: open dialogue, respectful distance, active interfaith engagement, live and let live, shared celebrations)
How important is access to interfaith dialogue programs and organizations?
How comfortable are you with religious symbols and practices different from yours in public spaces?
How important is religious diversity in your neighborhood and community?
How do you view the role of secularism and separation of church and state?
How willing are you to participate in local religious or cultural traditions that differ from your own? CULTURAL FLEX
How concerned are you about religious extremism or fundamentalism in your destination?
Rank these tolerance factors from most to least important: Community tolerance, Interfaith programs, Religious diversity, Secularism, Extremism concerns

Do you follow specific dietary laws or spiritual eating practices? (Select all that apply: halal, kosher, vegetarian/Hindu, Buddhist vegetarian, fasting periods, no alcohol, no pork, no beef, Jain diet, raw/natural food practices, none)
How important is access to religiously compliant food (halal, kosher, etc.) in daily life?
How important is access to specialty religious food stores and suppliers?
How important is having a dedicated space for prayer, meditation, or worship in your home?

Which religious observances affect your daily schedule or housing needs? (Select all that apply: daily prayer times, Sabbath/rest day, fasting periods, meditation schedule, modesty requirements, gender separation, noise/music restrictions, none)
How important is access to religious clothing and ritual items (prayer rugs, vestments, candles, incense, etc.)?
How important is it that your workplace accommodates religious practices (prayer breaks, holiday leave, dress code)?
How do you handle alcohol and substance use in social settings based on your beliefs? (Select one: strictly abstain, flexible depending on context, no restrictions, moderate personal choice)
How willing are you to adapt your religious lifestyle practices to fit local norms? CULTURAL FLEX
Rank these lifestyle factors from most to least important: Dietary compliance, Food access, Home worship space, Schedule accommodation, Workplace accommodation
How important is it to raise your children in your religious or spiritual tradition?
How important is access to faith-based schools or religious education for your children?

Which religious life cycle ceremonies are important for your family? (Select all that apply: birth/naming ceremonies, baptism/dedication, circumcision, bar/bat mitzvah, confirmation, first communion, coming-of-age rituals, religious marriage, funeral/burial rites, none)
How important is it that your children have same-faith peers and social groups?
How important is access to religious youth programs (youth groups, camps, retreats)?
How do you want to handle interfaith or non-religious relationships within your family?
How important is having religious role models and mentors available for your children?
How important is intergenerational religious knowledge transfer (grandparents teaching grandchildren faith traditions)?
How comfortable are you with your children being exposed to different religious perspectives in school?
Rank these family faith factors from most to least important: Religious upbringing, Faith-based schools, Life cycle ceremonies, Youth programs, Same-faith peers
How important is access to spiritual growth opportunities (retreats, pilgrimages, study groups)?
How important is access to spiritual mentorship or guidance from experienced practitioners?
How important is access to religious texts, literature, and study materials in your language?
How important is access to meditation, contemplation, or sacred nature spaces?

How do you prefer to contribute to your religious or spiritual community? (Select all that apply: volunteer service, financial tithing/donations, teaching/mentoring, worship leadership, community organizing, charitable outreach, none)
How important is digital access to religious services, teachings, and community (online worship, streaming, apps)?
How important is being able to share your faith or spiritual insights with others who are interested?
How important are spiritual crisis resources (pastoral counseling, faith-based support groups, chaplaincy)?
How important is that your spiritual practice integrates with your approach to health and wellness?
Rank these spiritual growth factors from most to least important: Growth opportunities, Mentorship, Sacred texts access, Digital access, Crisis resources
How willing are you to accept a smaller religious community in exchange for better overall quality of life?
How willing are you to travel further for worship services in exchange for a better neighborhood?
How willing are you to practice your faith primarily at home or online if local facilities are limited?
How willing are you to join a different denomination or branch of your faith if your specific tradition is unavailable?
How willing are you to accept limited religious dietary options if other food quality is excellent?
How willing are you to pioneer and help build a religious community where none currently exists?
How willing are you to accept less religious freedom in exchange for significantly better economic opportunities?
How willing are you to adapt your religious expression to be more private in a less tolerant environment? CULTURAL FLEX
How important is it that your destination's religious landscape is trending toward greater tolerance and diversity?

Rank these trade-off priorities from most to least important: Community size vs. quality of life, Travel distance for worship, Home practice vs. community, Denominational flexibility, Religious freedom vs. economics
How important is access to religiously appropriate funeral and burial practices?

Which end-of-life practices are important for your faith? (Select all that apply: traditional burial, cremation, sky burial, repatriation of remains, specific burial orientation, religious funeral rites, mourning period customs, memorial traditions, none specific)
How important is access to religious cemeteries or consecrated burial grounds?
How important is end-of-life spiritual care (chaplaincy, last rites, pastoral visits)?
How important is it that your destination respects religious mourning customs and bereavement practices?
How important is leaving a religious or spiritual legacy in your community?
How do you want your religious practice to evolve as you age? (Select one: deepen commitment, maintain current level, become more flexible, focus on spiritual over ritual, legacy/teaching focus)
How important is access to faith-based elder care and senior services?
How important is it that your family can continue your religious traditions after your passing?
Rank these end-of-life factors from most to least important: Funeral practices, Burial access, Spiritual care, Mourning customs, Religious legacy

Which religious issues are absolute dealbreakers that would eliminate a destination? (Select all that apply: no worship facilities for my faith, religious persecution, banned religious practices, no religious dietary options, no religious education, forced religious participation, no religious freedom laws, no clergy access)

Which religious features are non-negotiable must-haves? (Select all that apply: worship facility for my faith, religious freedom laws, faith community presence, religious education access, dietary compliance available, life cycle ceremony support, clergy/spiritual advisor access, interfaith tolerance)
On a scale of 0-100, how would you rank religion and spirituality as a priority compared to all other relocation factors?
What is your approximate annual budget for religious activities, donations, and faith-based expenses?
How important is it that the local culture is respectful and knowledgeable about your faith tradition?
How willing are you to be a religious and spiritual pioneer in a community that is unfamiliar with your faith?
How willing are you to embrace and learn from the spiritual traditions of your destination's local culture? CULTURAL FLEX
How important is it that your destination has a track record of religious harmony and peaceful coexistence?
How important is it that religious tolerance and freedom are improving in your destination?

Rank these overall religion categories from most to least important for your relocation: Personal beliefs & identity, Worship facilities & access, Religious freedom & legal protection, Tolerance & interfaith relations, Dietary & lifestyle requirements, Family & children, Spiritual growth, Religious trade-offs, End-of-life & legacy, Overall religious investment

modules.structure: 10 sections × 10 questions, each ending with a ranking question + 1 final master ranking (Q100)

questions.question: What is the maximum overall crime rate you would tolerate in your destination city?
Which types of crime are most concerning to you? (Select all that apply: violent crime, property crime, petty theft, organized crime, cybercrime, drug-related crime, hate crime, white-collar crime)
How important is it that crime rates in your destination have been declining over the past 5 years?
How important is it that your specific neighborhood has a lower crime rate than the city average?
How comfortable are you walking alone in your neighborhood after dark?
Which home security features do you require? (Select all that apply: gated community, security cameras, alarm system, doorman/concierge, reinforced doors/windows, safe room, smart locks)
How important is access to personal safety technology (emergency apps, GPS tracking, smart home security) in your daily life?
How important are community-based crime prevention programs (neighborhood watch, community policing) in your area?
How concerned are you about safety differences between daytime and nighttime in your area?
Rank these crime and personal safety factors from most to least important: Overall crime rate, Neighborhood-specific safety, Night safety, Home security features, Crime trend direction
What is the maximum acceptable emergency medical response time for your area?
What is the maximum acceptable distance from your home to the nearest emergency room?
How important is the quality and reliability of fire department services in your area?
How important is reliable and responsive police service in your neighborhood?
How important are multilingual emergency communication systems (e.g., 911/112 operators who speak your language)?
How important is access to advanced emergency services (helicopter medevac, specialized rescue teams, hazmat response)?
How important is your destination's proven capability to respond to natural disasters?
How important is a well-funded and organized government emergency management agency (like FEMA)?
How important is access to community emergency preparedness resources (evacuation plans, shelters, disaster kits)?
Rank these emergency service factors from most to least important: Medical response time, ER proximity, Fire services, Police reliability, Disaster response capability
How important is proximity to a Level I or Level II trauma center?
How important is 24/7 access to pharmacies and emergency medication in your area?
How important is the availability of medical specialists on-call for emergencies (cardiologist, neurologist, surgeon)?
How important is access to mental health crisis services (crisis hotlines, psychiatric emergency rooms, mobile crisis teams)?
How important is the availability of medical evacuation options (air ambulance, international medical transport) in your destination?
How important is community access to first aid training and self-care preparedness programs?
How important is access to telemedicine and virtual emergency consultations?
How important is it that hospitals in your area are internationally accredited (e.g., JCI accreditation)?
Does anyone in your household have specific emergency medical needs (chronic conditions, disabilities, allergies) that require specialized nearby care?
Rank these medical emergency factors from most to least important: Trauma center proximity, Pharmacy access, Specialist availability, Mental health crisis services, Hospital accreditation

Which police force characteristics are most important to you? (Select all that apply: community-oriented policing, low corruption, accountability/body cameras, diversity, professionalism, non-militarized, multilingual officers)
How important is the reliability and fairness of the local legal system?
How important is easy access to legal representation, including English-speaking lawyers?
How important is the availability of private security services (personal guards, private patrol, executive protection)?
How important are legal rights and protections specifically for foreign residents and expatriates?
How concerned are you about the availability of witness protection and whistleblower programs?
How important is access to reliable background check services for hiring domestic staff or business partners?
How important are victim support services (counseling, legal aid, financial assistance) for crime victims?
How concerned are you about white-collar crime protections (fraud, identity theft, financial scams targeting foreigners)?
Rank these law enforcement factors from most to least important: Police quality, Legal system fairness, Foreigner protections, Private security access, Crime victim support
How important are active neighborhood watch or community safety programs in your area?
How important is a strong sense of social cohesion and mutual trust among neighbors?
How important are school safety measures (security personnel, anti-bullying programs, safe school zones)?
How important is robust cyber safety infrastructure (identity theft protection, online fraud prevention, data privacy laws)?
How important are enforced workplace safety standards (OSHA-equivalent regulations, worker protections)?
How important is safety and security at public events and venues (concerts, sports, festivals)?
How important is child safety infrastructure (playground standards, child-proofing of public spaces, amber alert systems)?
How important are senior safety programs (fall prevention, elder abuse protections, senior-focused emergency services)?
How concerned are you about gender-based safety issues (harassment, domestic violence rates, gender-based violence protections)?
Rank these community safety factors from most to least important: Neighborhood cohesion, School safety, Cyber safety, Child safety infrastructure, Gender-based safety
How important is road safety (low accident rates, well-maintained roads, enforced traffic laws) in your destination?
How important is security on public transportation (cameras, staff presence, well-lit stations, emergency call points)?
How important are airport security and safety standards in your destination country?
How important is the safety and regulation of taxi and rideshare services (licensed drivers, GPS tracking, regulated fares)?
How important is pedestrian safety infrastructure (crosswalks, sidewalks, pedestrian zones, traffic calming)?
How concerned are you about motorcycle and scooter safety (helmet laws, lane discipline, accident rates)?
How important is maritime and water safety (coast guard, lifeguards, regulated water activities)?
How important is the safety record of alternative transportation (cable cars, trams, funiculars, ferries)?
How important are highway and interstate safety standards (guardrails, signage, emergency pull-offs, speed enforcement)?
Rank these transportation safety factors from most to least important: Road safety, Public transit security, Pedestrian safety, Rideshare safety, Maritime/water safety
How concerned are you about living in an area prone to natural disasters (earthquakes, hurricanes, floods, wildfires)?

Which specific natural hazards are you most concerned about? (Select all that apply: earthquakes, tsunamis, hurricanes/typhoons, tornadoes, flooding, wildfires, volcanic activity, landslides, drought)
How important are strict building safety codes and seismic standards in your destination?
How concerned are you about industrial and environmental hazards (chemical plants, pollution, toxic waste sites) near residential areas?
How concerned are you about endemic diseases and health hazards (malaria, dengue, waterborne illness) in your destination?
How concerned are you about wildlife and animal hazards (venomous snakes, dangerous predators, stray dogs, insect-borne diseases)?
How concerned are you about climate-related safety issues (extreme heat, severe storms, rising sea levels, air quality crises)?
How important is safe and clean drinking water quality and infrastructure?
How important are recreational safety standards (regulated adventure sports, lifeguarded beaches, safe hiking trails)?
Rank these environmental safety factors from most to least important: Natural disaster risk, Building codes, Disease/health hazards, Water quality, Climate-related safety
How important is reliable communication infrastructure during emergencies (cell service, internet, radio)?
How important are government emergency alert systems (text alerts, sirens, broadcast warnings)?
How important is national cybersecurity infrastructure (government cyber defense, regulated internet, anti-hacking laws)?
How important are strong data privacy protection laws (GDPR-equivalent, personal data rights, consent requirements)?
How important is network security and reliability (encrypted communications, secure banking, protected WiFi networks)?
How do you feel about the balance between public surveillance (CCTV, facial recognition) and personal privacy?
How important is access to emergency safety apps and technology (local emergency apps, safety check-in features, panic buttons)?
How important is reliable GPS and location services for personal safety and navigation?
How important is the security of financial transactions (chip+pin, two-factor authentication, fraud protection laws)?
Rank these technology safety factors from most to least important: Emergency communications, Cybersecurity laws, Data privacy, Surveillance-privacy balance, Financial transaction security
How important is cultural acceptance and safety for people of your background in your destination?
How important are LGBTQ+ safety protections and rights in your destination?
How important are women's safety protections and gender equality rights?
How important is religious freedom and safety to practice your faith openly?
How concerned are you about safety for ethnic and racial minorities in your destination?
How important are child protection services and laws (mandatory reporting, child welfare agencies, anti-exploitation laws)?
How concerned are you about alcohol and substance abuse culture and its impact on community safety?
How important is political stability and the absence of political violence or civil unrest?
How important is press freedom and access to reliable, uncensored information for your sense of safety?
Rank these cultural safety factors from most to least important: Cultural acceptance, LGBTQ+ safety, Women's safety, Religious freedom, Political stability
How much are you willing to invest personally in security measures (home security systems, private guards, insurance) per month?
How important is access to safety training and education programs (self-defense, first aid, disaster preparedness) in your area?
On a scale of 0-100, how would you rank overall safety as a priority compared to all other relocation factors?
How frequently would you review and adjust your personal safety measures after relocating?
How important is it that your destination meets international safety and security standards (Global Peace Index top 30, low terrorism risk)?

Which safety issues are absolute dealbreakers that would eliminate a destination? (Select all that apply: active conflict/war, high violent crime, terrorism risk, police corruption, no emergency services, political instability, no foreigner protections)

Which safety features are non-negotiable must-haves? (Select all that apply: reliable emergency services, low violent crime, stable government, safe public transit, clean drinking water, enforced building codes, data privacy laws)
How willing are you to accept slightly lower safety standards in exchange for other benefits (lower cost of living, better climate, cultural richness)?
How important is it that your destination is trending toward improved safety and security over the next 5-10 years?

Rank these overall safety categories from most to least important for your relocation: Crime & personal safety, Emergency services, Law enforcement, Community safety, Transportation safety, Environmental hazards, Cybersecurity, Cultural & social safety, Medical emergency infrastructure, Overall safety investment

modules.structure: 10 sections × 10 questions, each ending with a ranking question + 1 final master ranking (Q100)

questions.question: How important is legal abortion access in your destination country?
What level of abortion access do you require? (Select one: full unrestricted access, access with reasonable limits, access in medical emergencies only, not a factor for me, prefer restrictions)
How important is unrestricted access to contraception (birth control pills, IUDs, condoms, implants)?
How important is emergency contraception (morning-after pill) availability without prescription?

Which reproductive healthcare services are important for you to access? (Select all that apply: prenatal care, fertility treatments/IVF, genetic counseling, reproductive surgery, midwifery, surrogacy, egg/sperm donation, postpartum care, none)
How important is bodily autonomy protection in your destination's laws?
How important is legal protection for reproductive healthcare providers in your destination?
How important is insurance or public health coverage for reproductive healthcare?
How important is access to fertility treatments (IVF, egg freezing, surrogacy) regardless of marital status or sexual orientation?
Rank these reproductive rights factors from most to least important: Abortion access, Contraception access, Reproductive healthcare services, Bodily autonomy laws, Insurance coverage
How important is legal same-sex marriage recognition in your destination?
How important are legal protections against LGBTQ+ discrimination in employment, housing, and services?
What level of transgender legal recognition do you require? (Select one: full legal gender change without surgery, legal change with medical requirements, some recognition, not a factor for me)
How important is legal adoption and parenting equality for LGBTQ+ individuals and couples?
How important are hate crime protections based on sexual orientation and gender identity?
How important is it that conversion therapy is banned in your destination?
How important is legal recognition of civil partnerships and domestic partnerships?
How important are workplace equality protections specifically for LGBTQ+ individuals?
How important are legal protections for LGBTQ+ youth in schools (anti-bullying, inclusive curricula)?
Rank these LGBTQ+ rights factors from most to least important: Marriage equality, Anti-discrimination laws, Transgender recognition, Adoption equality, Hate crime protections
How important is freedom from government regulation of private consensual sexual conduct?
How important is legal protection for sexual minorities and alternative lifestyles (polyamory, kink, etc.)?
How important is protection of sexual privacy rights from government surveillance?
How do you feel about your destination's public decency and sexual expression laws?
How important is legal protection from sexual harassment in workplace and public spaces?
How important are strong sexual consent laws and enforcement?
How concerned are you about religious exemptions from sexual freedom protections in your destination?
How do you feel about regulation of sexual content in media and advertising?
How important is protection of digital sexual privacy (sexting laws, revenge porn laws, online harassment)?
Rank these sexual freedom factors from most to least important: Private conduct freedom, Sexual minority protections, Privacy rights, Harassment protections, Consent laws
What level of cultural conservatism versus liberalism do you prefer regarding sexuality in your destination?
How important is social acceptance of diverse sexual orientations in your community?
How comfortable are you with public displays of affection across different relationship types in your community?
How important is gender expression and role flexibility being accepted in your destination?
How important is body positivity and sexual diversity acceptance in local culture?
How do you feel about alternative relationship structures (polyamory, open relationships) being socially visible?
How important is feminist and gender equality integration in your destination's sexual culture?
How important is access to LGBTQ+-affirming religious or spiritual communities?
How willing are you to adapt your public expression of sexuality to local cultural norms? CULTURAL FLEX
Rank these cultural values factors from most to least important: Sexual liberalism/conservatism, Social acceptance, Gender flexibility, Body positivity, Feminist integration
How important is comprehensive sexual education in schools aligned with your values?
How important is access to sexual health clinics and STI testing facilities?
How important is access to gender-affirming healthcare services (hormone therapy, surgery, counseling)?
How important is sexual health insurance coverage (STI treatment, sexual wellness, gender-affirming care)?
How important is sexual health privacy in medical records and healthcare settings?
How important is access to sexual wellness and pleasure education through healthcare providers?
How important is access to HIV/AIDS prevention services (PrEP, PEP, testing, treatment)?
How important is access to sexual violence prevention, response services, and survivor support?
How important is sexual health information and contraception access for teenagers?
Rank these healthcare factors from most to least important: Sex education policy, Sexual health clinics, Gender-affirming care, HIV/AIDS services, Violence prevention

What is your preferred legal approach to sex work in your destination? (Select one: full legalization and regulation, decriminalization, Nordic model/buyer criminalization, full prohibition, no opinion)
How important is sex worker safety and labor protections in your destination?
How do you feel about the regulation and visibility of adult entertainment venues in your area?
How do you feel about online adult content regulation in your destination?
How important is anti-trafficking enforcement and victim protection in your destination?
How important is mandatory STI testing and health regulation for the sex industry?
How concerned are you about proximity to red-light districts or adult entertainment zones?
How important is sex offender registration and community notification laws in your destination?
How important is child protection from sexual exploitation and online predation?
Rank these regulation factors from most to least important: Sex work legal framework, Worker safety, Anti-trafficking, Sex offender registry, Child protection
How important is the availability of dating apps and online matchmaking platforms in your destination?
How easy do you need it to be for a foreigner or expat to enter the local dating scene?
How important is social acceptance of interracial, interethnic, or intercultural romantic relationships?
How important is it that the local culture supports autonomous partner choice rather than family-arranged matchmaking?
How important is an active social and nightlife scene for meeting potential partners (bars, social clubs, meetup groups)?
How concerned are you about social stigma or pressure around being single, unmarried, or child-free?
How important is personal safety while dating as a foreigner (romance scams, targeting of expats)?
How important is social acceptance of dating as a single parent or after divorce?
How willing are you to adapt to local dating customs and courtship norms? CULTURAL FLEX
Rank these dating factors from most to least important: Dating accessibility, Intercultural acceptance, Autonomous partner choice, Social scene, Dating safety
How important is social and legal acceptance of cohabitation before marriage?
How important is social acceptance of diverse family structures (single parents, blended families, same-sex parents, child-free couples)?
How important are equitable divorce laws and processes in your destination?
How important is social acceptance of age-gap romantic relationships?
How important is social acceptance of interfaith or non-religious romantic relationships?
How important is the availability of relationship counseling, couples therapy, and mediation services?
How important is that social venues and community events cater to diverse age groups for meeting people?
How important is the local culture's support for maintaining friendships and social connections outside of a romantic partnership?
How willing are you to accept different relationship and family norms than your home culture? CULTURAL FLEX
Rank these relationship factors from most to least important: Cohabitation acceptance, Diverse family acceptance, Divorce equity, Counseling access, Social connection culture
How willing are you to accept more conservative sexual norms in exchange for better overall quality of life?
How willing are you to accept limited LGBTQ+ legal protections in exchange for other important benefits?
How willing are you to accept limited reproductive healthcare access in exchange for lower cost of living?
How willing are you to be more private about your sexual identity or lifestyle in a less progressive environment? CULTURAL FLEX
How willing are you to accept different gender role expectations in exchange for cultural richness?
How concerned are you about the direction your destination is trending on sexual rights (progressive vs. regressive)?
How important is it that your destination is actively improving its sexual rights and freedoms?
How willing are you to be an advocate or activist for sexual rights in a less progressive environment?
How important is it that the local expat community shares your values on sexual rights and freedoms?

Rank these trade-off priorities from most to least important: Conservative norms vs. quality of life, LGBTQ+ rights vs. other benefits, Reproductive access vs. cost, Gender roles vs. culture, Rights trajectory

Which sexual rights issues are absolute dealbreakers that would eliminate a destination? (Select all that apply: criminalized homosexuality, no abortion access, no contraception access, no LGBTQ+ protections, forced marriage legal, no sexual harassment laws, no gender-affirming healthcare, criminalized adultery, death penalty for sexual offenses, no divorce rights)

Which sexual rights features are non-negotiable must-haves? (Select all that apply: marriage equality, LGBTQ+ anti-discrimination, abortion access, contraception access, sexual harassment protections, gender-affirming healthcare, consent laws, sexual privacy protections, dating freedom, divorce rights)
On a scale of 0-100, how would you rank sexual beliefs, practices, and laws as a priority compared to all other relocation factors?
How important is it that the local culture's sexual values broadly align with your own?
How important is living in a destination known internationally as progressive on sexual rights?
How willing are you to embrace and understand the local culture's approach to sexuality, even if different from yours? CULTURAL FLEX
How important is access to sexual and reproductive health services in your language?
How important is it that your children grow up in a sexually inclusive and educated environment?
How important is it that your destination's sexual rights landscape is improving and trending upward?

Rank these overall sexual beliefs categories from most to least important for your relocation: Reproductive rights, LGBTQ+ legal rights, Sexual freedom & privacy, Cultural values & acceptance, Sexual healthcare & education, Sex work regulation, Dating culture, Relationship & family structure, Sexual beliefs trade-offs, Overall sexual rights investment

modules.structure: 10 sections x 10 questions (Q1-Q99) + 1 final cross-section ranking (Q100)

questions.question: How often do you shop for groceries?
Which grocery store types do you prefer?
How far are you willing to travel for your preferred grocery store or market?
How important is having multiple grocery options within a 15-minute radius?
Is 24-hour or extended-hours grocery access important to you?
Do you prefer local or imported food products?
Which household essentials do you buy regularly?
Would you pay 15-25% more for eco-friendly or sustainable packaging on everyday items?
When shopping for everyday items, how much does price influence your decisions compared to brand?
Rank these essentials from most to least important: Grocery variety, Price affordability, Proximity/convenience, Organic/sustainable options, 24-hour access
How often do you buy new clothing items?
Which clothing categories are most important to your wardrobe?
What percentage of your monthly income do you typically spend on clothing?
Do you prefer in-store shopping (for trying on items) over online shopping for clothing?
How important will access to international fashion brands be in the future location?
Which accessories do you shop for regularly?
How important is the availability of tailoring or alteration services?
Do you prefer following seasonal fashion trends or timeless/classic styles?
Would you travel to another city or country for a major fashion purchase?
Rank these fashion factors from most to least important: Brand selection, Price affordability, Quality/durability, Tailoring services, Style relevance
How often do you purchase luxury items?
Which luxury categories interest you most?
Do you prefer shopping at flagship brand stores or multi-brand luxury boutiques?
Is duty-free shopping availability important for your luxury purchases?
Would you buy luxury goods locally even if prices are 15-30% higher than abroad?
How important is brand prestige as a status symbol in your luxury purchases?
Which luxury services do you value?
Would you consider purchasing certified authenticated pre-owned luxury goods?
How important is potential resale value when deciding on a luxury purchase?
Rank these luxury priorities from most to least important: Brand prestige, Service quality, Price/value, Exclusivity/rarity, Resale value
How often do you buy or lease a new vehicle?
Which vehicle types interest you?
Do you prefer buying new vehicles or certified pre-owned?
How important is parking availability in your vehicle purchase decision?
Would you import a vehicle from abroad for better options or cost savings?
How important is brand reputation for reliability in your vehicle purchases?
Which vehicle-related services are essential?
What is your preferred method for purchasing a vehicle?
How important is projected resale value in your vehicle selection?
Rank these vehicle priorities from most to least important: Brand reputation, Price affordability, Service/maintenance, Local availability, Resale value
How often do you buy home furnishings or decor items?
Which home goods categories are most important?
Do you prefer ready-made furniture or custom/bespoke pieces?
Where do you prefer to shop for home goods?
Would you import home goods from abroad if local options are limited?
How important is matching design styles to your home's aesthetic?
Which home goods services do you value?
